test_cases.test_case: Sign in with valid registered email and valid password
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and accessing the sign-in page with a registered account credential to provide.
A registered account exists for <registered email> with a known password <valid password>
steps: 1. Enter <registered email> in the Email/Username field
2. Enter <valid password> in the Password field (meets minimum length and character requirements)
3. Click the 'Sign In' button
test_cases.expected_result: A flash 'Signed in successfully.' is displayed; the Accounts Overview page opens showing the heading 'Accounts Overview'; the left-hand navigation is visible and includes 'Open New Account', 'Transfer Funds', 'Payments', and 'Request Loan'.

test_cases.test_case: Sign in with valid registered username and valid password
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and accessing the sign-in page with a registered account credential to provide.
A registered account exists for <registered username> with a known password <valid password>
steps: 1. Enter <registered username> in the Email/Username field
2. Enter <valid password> in the Password field (meets minimum length and character requirements)
3. Click the 'Sign In' button
test_cases.expected_result: A flash 'Signed in successfully.' is displayed; the Accounts Overview page opens showing the heading 'Accounts Overview'; the left-hand navigation is visible and includes 'Open New Account', 'Transfer Funds', 'Payments', and 'Request Loan'.

test_cases.test_case: Navigate to Password Reset via 'Forgot Password?' link
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and accessing the sign-in page with a registered account credential to provide.
steps: 1. Click the 'Forgot Password?' link on the login form
test_cases.expected_result: The Password Reset page opens showing a password reset form prompting for Email or Username and a control to submit the reset request (for example a 'Submit' or 'Send reset' button).

test_cases.test_case: Leave Email/Username blank and attempt Sign In
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the Sign In page
steps: 1. Leave the Email/Username field blank
2. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
3. Click the Sign In button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Email/Username field indicating it is required; the form does not submit and the user is not signed in

test_cases.test_case: Leave Password blank and attempt Sign In
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the Sign In page
steps: 1. Enter <valid email or username> in the Email/Username field
2. Leave the Password field blank
3. Click the Sign In button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Password field indicating it is required; the form does not submit and the user is not signed in

test_cases.test_case: Submit with all required fields empty
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the Sign In page
steps: 1. Leave the Email/Username field blank
2. Leave the Password field blank
3. Click the Sign In button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation errors appear on both Email/Username and Password fields indicating they are required; the form does not submit and the user is not signed in

test_cases.test_case: Enter an invalid email format in Email/Username when providing an email
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the Sign In page
steps: 1. Enter <invalid email format> in the Email/Username field
2. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
3. Click the Sign In button
test_cases.expected_result: Email/Username field displays an inline validation error indicating it must be a valid email address; the form does not submit and the user is not signed in

test_cases.test_case: Password fails policy (too short) and Sign In is attempted
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the Sign In page
steps: 1. Enter <valid email or username> in the Email/Username field
2. Enter <password shorter than minimum length> in the Password field
3. Click the Sign In button
test_cases.expected_result: Password field displays an inline validation error indicating it does not meet the password policy (minimum length 8 and required character types); the form does not submit and the user is not signed in

test_cases.test_case: Submit with incorrect credentials (authentication failure)
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the Sign In page
steps: 1. Enter <registered email or username> in the Email/Username field
2. Enter <incorrect password> in the Password field
3. Click the Sign In button
test_cases.expected_result: The page displays 'Incorrect email or password. Please try again.'; the Password field is cleared; the user remains unauthenticated and is not redirected

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to access Sign In while already authenticated (precondition not met)
preconditions: User is already authenticated and on the Accounts Overview page
steps: 1. Sign in as <registered account> and arrive at Accounts Overview
2. Navigate to the Sign In page (e.g., enter the sign-in URL or click Sign In link)
3. Observe the resulting page
test_cases.expected_result: Sign In form is not displayed; the user remains on Accounts Overview (or is redirected to Accounts Overview) and no Sign In action is presented, indicating the Sign In precondition is enforced

test_cases.test_case: Password at minimum length with required character classes succeeds
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the sign-in page
There is a registered account whose Email/Username and Password exactly match the credentials to be entered
steps: 1. Enter <registered Email or Username> in the Email_Username field
2. Enter a Password that is exactly <minimum length> characters and includes at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one number, and one special character in the Password field
3. Click Sign In
test_cases.expected_result: Sign-in succeeds: the UI flashes 'Signed in successfully.' and the Accounts Overview page is displayed with the left-hand navigation unlocked

test_cases.test_case: Password one character shorter than minimum is blocked (length boundary)
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the sign-in page
steps: 1. Enter <registered Email or Username> in the Email_Username field
2. Enter a Password that is exactly <one unit below minimum length> characters but otherwise includes uppercase, lowercase, and a number and special character in the Password field
3. Click Sign In
test_cases.expected_result: Sign-in is blocked: the Password field displays an inline validation error indicating the password does not meet the minimum length requirement and no redirect to Accounts Overview occurs

test_cases.test_case: Password missing required special character is blocked (complexity boundary)
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the sign-in page
steps: 1. Enter <registered Email or Username> in the Email_Username field
2. Enter a Password that meets the minimum length and contains uppercase, lowercase, and a numeric character but contains no special character in the Password field
3. Click Sign In
test_cases.expected_result: Sign-in is blocked: the Password field displays an inline validation error indicating the password does not meet complexity requirements and no redirect to Accounts Overview occurs

test_cases.test_case: Email-like value with invalid email format is blocked (email-format boundary)
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the sign-in page
steps: 1. Enter a value in the Email_Username field that is intended to be an email but is missing a required email component (e.g., missing top-level domain) so it fails valid-email format
2. Enter a Password that otherwise meets all password constraints in the Password field
3. Click Sign In
test_cases.expected_result: Sign-in is blocked: the Email_Username field displays an inline validation error indicating the value is not a valid email format and no redirect to Accounts Overview occurs

test_cases.test_case: Leading and trailing whitespace in Email/Username is trimmed and sign-in succeeds
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the sign-in page
There is a registered account whose Email/Username and Password match the credentials to be entered (without surrounding whitespace)
steps: 1. Enter <registered Email or Username> with leading and trailing whitespace characters in the Email_Username field
2. Enter a Password that meets all password constraints in the Password field
3. Click Sign In
test_cases.expected_result: Sign-in succeeds: leading/trailing whitespace is trimmed from the Email_Username value before authentication, the UI flashes 'Signed in successfully.' and the Accounts Overview page is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click of Sign In with valid credentials results in a single successful sign-in UI flow
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the sign-in page
There is a registered account whose Email/Username and Password match the credentials to be entered
steps: 1. Enter <registered Email or Username> in the Email_Username field
2. Enter a Password that meets all password constraints in the Password field
3. Double-click the Sign In button (two quick clicks in succession)
test_cases.expected_result: Sign-in succeeds once: the UI displays a single flash 'Signed in successfully.' and the Accounts Overview page is loaded; the sign-in form is not re-displayed or submitted a second time

test_cases.test_case: Successful registration with all valid inputs redirects to Login with success message
preconditions: Unauthenticated visitor on the sign-in page intending to create a new customer account
steps: 1. Click the Register link on the sign-in page to open the Register page
2. Enter <valid first name> in the First Name field
3. Enter <valid last name> in the Last Name field
4. Enter <valid street address> in the Street Address field
5. Enter <valid city> in the City field
6. Select <State> from the State dropdown
7. Enter <valid ZIP in 5+4 format> in the ZIP Code field
8. Enter <valid US phone digits> in the Phone Number field
9. Enter <valid SSN digits> in the Social Security Number field
10. Enter <valid email> in the Username field
11. Enter <valid password of at least 8 characters> in the Password field
12. Enter the same <valid password> in the Confirm Password field
13. Click the Register button
test_cases.expected_result: Account created successfully — please sign in; redirect to login page

test_cases.test_case: Phone Number auto-formats to US pattern as user types
preconditions: Unauthenticated visitor on the sign-in page intending to create a new customer account
steps: 1. Click the Register link on the sign-in page to open the Register page
2. Focus the Phone Number field
3. Type the digits of a 10-digit US phone number into the Phone Number field (digits only, no separators)
4. Observe the Phone Number field while typing
test_cases.expected_result: Phone Number field displays the entered digits formatted to the US pattern (123) 456-7890 (i.e., auto-formats to the pattern ^\(\d{3}\) \d{3}-\d{4}$) and no phone-format validation error is shown

test_cases.test_case: Social Security Number auto-formats to 123-45-6789 pattern as user types
preconditions: Unauthenticated visitor on the sign-in page intending to create a new customer account
steps: 1. Click the Register link on the sign-in page to open the Register page
2. Focus the Social Security Number field
3. Type the digits of a 9-digit SSN into the Social Security Number field (digits only, no separators)
4. Observe the Social Security Number field while typing
test_cases.expected_result: Social Security Number field displays the entered digits formatted to the pattern 123-45-6789 (i.e., auto-formats to the pattern ^\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}$) and no SSN-format validation error is shown

test_cases.test_case: ZIP Code accepts 5-digit and 5+4 formats and displays formatted value without validation error
preconditions: Unauthenticated visitor on the sign-in page intending to create a new customer account
steps: 1. Click the Register link on the sign-in page to open the Register page
2. Enter <valid 5-digit ZIP> in the ZIP Code field and observe there is no inline validation error
3. Replace the ZIP Code value with <valid ZIP in 5+4 format> and observe the field
4. Verify no ZIP Code validation error is displayed
test_cases.expected_result: ZIP Code field accepts and displays the entered value in either 12345 or 12345-6789 format (matching pattern ^\d{5}(-\d{4})?$) and no inline ZIP validation error is shown

test_cases.test_case: Accounts table displays masked numbers, types, balances, status badges, open dates, and total footer
preconditions: User logged in as <Customer>
One or more accounts exist for the customer
steps: 1. Click Accounts Overview in the left-hand navigation to open the Accounts Overview page
2. Observe the Accounts Overview landing page
test_cases.expected_result: Welcome message displays the customer's name '<Customer full name>'; the Accounts table is visible and lists each account row showing: Account Number in masked format '****<last 4 digits>', Account Type, Current Balance, an 'Active' status badge, and Open Date. Rows are ordered by Open Date (earliest first). The table footer displays a Total Balance equal to the sum of the Current Balances shown in the table.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-clicking Send Message results in a single submission
preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Support Center page
Secure Message form is visible
steps: 1. Enter a valid Subject
2. Select a Category
3. Enter a valid, non-empty value in Message_Body
4. Click Send Message twice in rapid succession
test_cases.expected_result: Only a single success outcome is shown: the page displays one 'Message sent successfully.' with a single ticket ID; the second click is ignored or blocked and no duplicate success message or second ticket ID appears — is blocked / error shown for the duplicate attempt.

module_context.summary: The Login module authenticates users by validating Email/Username and Password credentials and directing successful sign-ins to the Accounts Overview.
module_context.where_it_fits: This module is the application's entry point for returning users on the initial sign-in page; upon successful authentication it redirects to the Accounts Overview and unlocks the left-hand navigation (Open New Account, Transfer Funds, Payments, Request Loan, etc.).
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is unauthenticated and accessing the sign-in page with a registered account credential to provide.

module_context.summary: A user sign-up form that collects personal, contact, and credential information with validation rules for email, phone, ZIP, SSN, and password before creating a new account.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the initial sign-in page via the Register link, this module is part of the onboarding flow that precedes access to the account dashboard and left-hand navigation options (Accounts Overview, Open New Account, Transfer Funds, etc.). It runs during account creation and redirects back to the Login page on successful registration.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Unauthenticated visitor on the sign-in page intending to create a new customer account

module_context.summary: Displays a personalized dashboard table of the customer's accounts with masked account numbers, account types, current balances, statuses, open dates, and a total balance footer.
module_context.where_it_fits: Serves as the default dashboard view after a successful sign-in (Accounts Overview in the left-hand navigation). It is the primary authenticated landing page and hub from which users access features like Open New Account, Transfer Funds, Payments, and other account services.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated (logged-in) customer session with one or more accounts associated to the user.

module_context.summary: Provides a form to open a new Checking or Savings account by selecting an account type, specifying an initial deposit and funding source, and validating requirements before creation.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation under 'Open New Account' after successful sign-in and from the logged-in account dashboard; it is part of the account setup flow and on success redirects the user back to Accounts Overview. It sits alongside other post-login features like Transfer Funds, Bill Pay, and Manage Cards.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and has at least one existing funding account (with sufficient balance) available to fund the new account.

module_context.summary: Allows a logged-in customer to move money between their own Parabank accounts or to/from an external account with validation and transaction confirmation.
module_context.where_it_fits: This module is accessed from the authenticated user's left-hand navigation as option ③ Transfer Funds and is part of post-login account management workflows, typically used after viewing the Accounts Overview dashboard. It operates alongside other account actions like Open New Account, Payments, and Account Statements.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in as a customer and has at least one Checking or Savings account available as a source; for external transfers the user has the external account details to enter and confirm.

module_context.summary: Provides a bill-payment form that collects payee and payment details, validates entries (including account-number confirmation and available funds), executes payments, returns a reference code on success, and updates account balances.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation as the Bill Pay (Payments) option (menu item ④) after successful sign-in and from the account dashboard; it is used during the post-login transaction flow to move funds to external payees and adjust account balances.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated customer session with at least one funded source account available and the user on the account dashboard or navigation menu.

module_context.summary: Provides an interface for applying for Personal, Auto, or Home loans with amount and down-payment validation, collateral selection and simulated credit approval that returns approval or denial reasons.
module_context.where_it_fits: Available from the left-hand navigation as option ⑤ Request Loan after successful login and arrival at the account dashboard (Accounts Overview). It is part of the account services flow where users submit credit applications and, if approved, a loan account is created and shown alongside other accounts.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and on the dashboard with at least one existing account available to select as collateral and basic customer/credit profile data present in the system.

module_context.summary: Provides an editable customer profile form pre-filled with name, address, and phone fields that validates input and displays success or inline error feedback upon submission.
module_context.where_it_fits: This module is reachable from the left-hand navigation as "Update Contact Info" on the account dashboard that users see after a successful sign-in; it is part of account maintenance workflows alongside items like Manage Cards and Security Settings.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in as an authenticated customer and is on the account dashboard with an existing customer profile.

module_context.summary: Provides customers the ability to request new debit or credit cards and to modify controls on existing cards such as spending limits, travel notices, and card status.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation as Manage Cards after a successful sign-in and landing on the account dashboard (left menu item ⑦ Manage Cards). It is part of post-login account management alongside options like Accounts Overview and Security Settings for managing payment instruments and related controls.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated customer session with at least one active account; existing card(s) present if the user intends to update controls.

module_context.summary: Displays a portfolio snapshot and provides forms to execute same-day buy/sell trades and to create recurring investment plans with inline validation and success confirmations.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation as the Investments option after successful sign-in, it complements the Accounts Overview and other account-management features (e.g., Transfer Funds, Manage Cards) by handling investment holdings and trade activity within the user's account lifecycle. Actions on this page update the user's holdings and are reflected in portfolio data shown across the application.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in as a customer with an active account and at least one funding/destination account available for trades.

module_context.summary: Provides generation of account statements for a selected period and account, and management of e-statement (paperless) preferences including email validation and success/failure messaging.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation under 'Account Statements' after successful sign-in (the dashboard/Accounts Overview is the default view); it is part of the post-login account management tools alongside items like Accounts Overview, Open New Account, and Security Settings. This module is used when a logged-in customer needs historical transaction records or to change e-statement delivery preferences.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is authenticated (logged in) and has at least one active account available to select from; user is on the post-login dashboard with left-hand navigation visible.

module_context.summary: Security Settings provides a form for changing the user's password with current-password verification, strong-password enforcement, match checking, and validation feedback.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation as the 'Security Settings' option (item 10) on the post-login dashboard, this module is part of the account management and security area alongside Update Contact Info, Manage Cards, and Account Statements. It is used during an authenticated user's lifecycle when they need to update credential security.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is authenticated and has an active session on their account dashboard

module_context.summary: Support Center provides secure message and callback-request forms for contacting customer support, with field validation, attachment handling, ticket ID confirmation for messages, and email confirmation for callback requests.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation as 'Support Center' (option ⑪) after successful sign-in and landing on the account dashboard; it serves as the in-app channel for users to report issues or request callbacks within the normal account management flow.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated customer on their account dashboard with the left-hand navigation available.
